modalFeedbackSubtitle: Share your thoughts — I read everything
modalThanksSubtitle: Your support keeps this project alive

devStory: Hey! I'm an independent developer who built OpenGSC as a free tool for everyone.
devStoryDetail: No VC funding, no team, no ads — just me and a lot of coffee. If this tool saves you time or money, a small donation means a lot and helps keep development going.
optContentDecayDesc: Find content that needs to be updated.
optCannibalizationDesc: Find pages that are competing against each other.
optStrikingDesc: Find keywords you can easily rank for.
optCtrDesc: See if your titles are performing well.
cdmHowItWorks1: The heatmap graph shows how each webpage performs over time, using color shading to highlight trends.
cdmHowItWorks2: It lets you quickly see which pages consistently do well and which ones might need updating to improve.
cdmHowItWorks3: This visual overview makes it easy to spot patterns across many pages and months, helping you focus your SEO work where it's needed most.
cdmWhatToDo1: Select a time frame that you want to target (we recommend 16 months).
cdmWhatToDo2: Set threshold to where you can start to see a wide range of color shades.
cdmWhatToDo3: Look for pages that used to perform well that have since dropped off or high performing pages to replicate in other ways.
cdmWhatToDo4: Pages that are refreshed or add new content by at least 30% have a significant increase in traffic on average.
cdmTableTitle: Decaying Pages That May Need Refreshing
kcHowItWorks1: This report highlights the queries where multiple URLs are in competition with each other.
kcHowItWorks3: Pages representing less than 10% of the total query impressions are excluded
kcHowItWorks4: Queries where the top two pages are more than 20 positions apart are excluded
kcWhatToDo1: Start by looking at the top query for each competing page. If both pages share the same top query, it's likely cannibalization — consider consolidating into a single, stronger page.
kcWhatToDo2: If the top queries are different, check the SERPs for each query. If results overlap, one page can probably rank for both. If the SERPs are distinct, you likely need separate pages.
kcWhatToDo3: Every case is different — review each issue individually before making changes.
kcEmptyDesc: There are no queries with multiple pages competing.
sdkPosRangeDesc: Only show queries ranking between these positions.
sdkWhatToDo1: Use this report to find queries that are close to ranking on the first page of Google.
sdkWhatToDo2: Improve the content of ranking pages to increase the position for these queries.
sdkEmptyDesc: There are no queries matching your filters.
ctrHowItWorks2: have shown that the first position on Google search results has an average of 27.6% CTR, which drops to 15.8% for the second position, etc.
ctrHowItWorks3: We compare your numbers against these benchmarks to help you understand which URLs are underperforming.
ctrHowItWorks4: Only queries in the top 10 positions are included in this report.
ctrImprove1: Write better meta descriptions and titles
ctrImprove2: Title tags between 40 and 60 chars have the best CTR
ctrImprove3: Positive titles have better CTR than negative or neutral
ctrImprove4: Improve your position on Google search results
ctrEmptyDesc: There are no queries in the top 10 positions yet.
setHelpTitle: The settings tab that actually helps your analysis
setHelpDesc: Set up these essential workflows in 2 minutes to automatically find your biggest SEO opportunities, track what's actually working, and create reports that impress clients.
setBrandMonitorDesc: Stop confusing brand searches with actual SEO wins—see your true organic performance.
setContentGroupsDesc: Group content to see what part of your site is growing and what's decaying.
setClientPortalDesc: Turn any report into a live, shareable link your clients can access anytime.
setBrandedDesc1: Set up branded keywords for {domain} to monitor your brand's performance in search results.
setBrandedDesc2: We recommend adding branded keywords that are unique to your brand, and are not generic terms.
setBrandedDesc3: We use Broad Match / Contains statement for these keywords. If you add ikea, it will match queries like ikea chair or ikea table.
setTopicDesc1: Topic Cluster is a collection of related queries or keywords.
setTopicDesc2: Grouping keywords allows you to analyze their performance much more efficiently than analyzing individually.
setContentDesc1: Grouping related pages allows you to analyze the performance of multiple pages much more efficiently.
setContentDesc2: Common use cases include a group for all blog posts, pages related to a similar topic, multiple landing pages, or programmatic SEO pages.
setSharedLinkDesc1: Share the {domain} dashboard with your clients using a public shared link. Here's an
setSharedLinkDesc3: You can also embed the shared link into reporting platforms, such as
setSharedLinkNote: Shared Links are impossible to guess, and you can remove them at any time.

setSuperSiteDesc2: We check your sitemaps every 24 hours to discover new pages.
setUnlockSelection: and unlock Super Site Page Selection

syncStarted: Data sync started in background! It may take a few minutes to fetch all data.
setupDesc: Automatically generate Topic Clusters and Content Groups from your GSC data using AI.
aiProviderDesc: Choose an AI provider to generate clusters. AI models understand semantic meaning better than algorithmic rules. If left blank, it will try to use the server's environment variables or fall back to an algorithmic mode.

renameHintGroup: Click a name to rename it. Edit URL patterns (pipe-separated). Uncheck to exclude.
aiConfigDesc: Configure your preferred AI provider to power features like automatic topic clustering and content grouping. Keys are saved securely in your browser.
claritySettingsNote: Token is configured per-site in the «UX / Clarity» tab.
claritySubtitle: Analyze user behavior: dead clicks, rage clicks, scroll depth, and problem pages.
clarityStep1: Sign up at clarity.microsoft.com — it's completely free.
clarityStep2: Create a new project and install the tracking script on your site.
clarityStep3: Wait 1–3 days for Clarity to collect user behavior data.
clarityStep4: Go to Settings → Data Export → Generate new API token and copy the token.
clarityStep5: Paste the token and Project ID below and click «Save».
clarityStep6: Click «Load data» — your metrics will appear right away.
clarityTokenPlaceholder: Bearer token from Settings → Data Export
clarityNoData: No data yet. Save your token and click «Load data».
clarityNotConfigured: Token not configured. Fill in the fields below.
clarityApiLimit: API limit: 10 requests per day, data for the last 1–3 days.
clarityMetricDeadClicksDesc: Clicks on elements with no page response
clarityMetricQuickbackDesc: User immediately navigated back after a click
clarityMetricExcessiveScrollDesc: Users scrolled frantically — a sign they were lost on the page
clarityMetricScrollDesc: Average % of page users scroll through
clarityWhatIsText: Clarity is a free Microsoft tool for recording user sessions and analyzing behavior. It shows what users click, how they scroll, and where they lose interest. Essential for CRO — Conversion Rate Optimization.
clarityWhyTitle: Why does an SEO specialist need this?
clarityWhyText: Dead clicks point to broken CTAs. Rage clicks signal bugs or poor UX. Quickback clicks mean a page doesn't match user intent. Low scroll depth means content below the fold isn't being read. All of this directly affects behavioral signals and conversion rates.
seoSubtitle: SERP analysis → structure, text and analytics for AI search
indexerSubtitle: Subdomain bot farm, crawler statistics, queue, and content sandbox
indexerTabDescStats: Crawl summary dashboard showing search engine bots activity. Track bot requests (Google, Yandex, Bing, Mail.ru), 304 Not Modified responses, and crawl budget trends across your doorway network.
indexerTabDescLogs: Real-time log stream of search crawler hits. Monitor incoming User-Agents, IP addresses, requested URLs, and HTTP response codes in real-time.
indexerTabDescQueue: Target money site URLs queued for crawling and indexing. These links are automatically mashing up and crosslinking inside the doorway subdomains.
indexerTabDescDomains: Manage your doorway network domains. Add custom drop domains or new registrations, select markup templates, and define your redirect targets.
indexerTabDescLinks: Visual crosslinking topology map. Shows link weight distribution and semantic routes between your doorway domains and money pages.
indexerTabDescDict: Keyword wordlists used by the doorway generator. Helps generate messy semantic text variations to attract crawlers naturally.
indexerTabDescSettings: Configure your deployment endpoint URL and copy the cloaking index.php script for your doorway servers.
indexerHelpTitle: Setup Guide: Deploying Doorway Farms & Infinite Crawling
indexerHelpIntro: Follow this structural configuration guide to set up endless subdomain doorways that redirect real visitors while logging search engine crawls back to OpenGSC.
indexerStep1Text: Go to your domain registrar (like Cloudflare, Namecheap, or GoDaddy). Add a new DNS record: set Type to 'A', Name to '*' (asterisk), and Value to your server's public IP address. This wildcard setting tells the DNS system that any subdomain requested (e.g., shop.yourdomain.com or blog.yourdomain.com) should resolve to your hosting server.
indexerStep2Text: Your server must route all requests to the single index.php file. For Shared Hosting (Apache): create a file named '.htaccess' in your root folder and insert two lines: 'RewriteEngine On' and 'RewriteRule ^(.*)$ index.php [L,QSA]'. For VPS (Nginx): add 'try_files $uri $uri/ /index.php?$query_string;' inside your site location config block and restart Nginx (sudo systemctl restart nginx).
indexerStep3Text: Copy the PHP code generated below. Create a file named 'index.php' in your server's root folder (via cPanel File Manager or FTP tools like FileZilla), paste the code, and save. Make sure the PHP cURL extension is enabled on your hosting (it is enabled on 99% of servers by default) so the script can ping your OpenGSC dashboard.
indexerStep4Text: In the 'Domains' tab in OpenGSC, make sure to add your 'Money Redirect Target URL' (the real project you want to drive traffic or index backlinks to). The script will automatically cloak the site: showing text sandboxes with dictionary keywords to Googlebot, while instantly redirecting human users to your money project.
indexerNoDataDesc: You have not added any domains or received any traffic yet. Go to the "Domains" tab to link your first domain and configure tracking according to the instructions in "Settings".
indexerNoLogsDesc: Ensure you have active domains and they are receiving search engine crawler visits.
indexerNoLinksDesc: Add domains to your farm to view the connection topology map.
linksDesc: Configure how doorways in your bot farm link to each other to spread crawl budget and avoid search footprint.
linksRingDesc: Each site links to the next one in a circle. Avoids footprints, looks natural.
linksMeshDesc: Complex cross-linking with multiple overlapping links for aggressive indexing.
linksPyramidDesc: Low-quality doorways pass authority to mid-tier satellites, pointing to target money domain.
linksAlertCopied: Cross-linking HTML code copied to clipboard!
linksNoDomainsDesc: Add domains to your farm to view the connection topology map.
linksFooterNote: The doorway PHP script will automatically read the links map and inject cross-domain references into generated templates dynamically to direct crawl authority across nodes.
dictAiDesc: Automatically seed your doorway dictionaries using your AI API Provider keys.
dictBulkPlaceholder:
best sneakers sale
cheap flight tickets
bitcoin mining pool
instant loan approval
dictClearConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all keywords from the dictionary? doorways will fall back to default keywords.
dictAiSuccess: AI generated and saved {count} keywords for niche: {niche}.
dictEmptyDesc: doorway generators will fall back to generic ecommerce keywords until you seed the pool.
settPublicUrlDesc: Used to tell the PHP script where to send cURL logs. Do not include trailing slashes.
settPreFillDesc: Pre-fills credentials and redirect targets in the PHP script copybox below.
settNoDomainsWarning: Please add at least one domain to your farm to configure credentials.
settCloakingDesc: Human visits triggers redirection webhook and redirects to target money site. Bots receive keyword mash pages (served with ETag + 200/304 response) and send crawl logs webhook to OpenGSC.
seoErrNoSerpKey: No SERP provider key — add it in Settings.
seoErrNoAiKey: No AI provider key — add it in Settings → AI Providers.
seoErrParseJson: Model returned invalid JSON — try again or another provider.
seoErrParseJsonShort: Model returned invalid JSON — try again.
seoErrFillKwUrl: Fill in the keyword and the page URL.
seoKeywordPh: e.g. best running shoes for beginners
seoFillManually: ⚠ Fill in manually with real data:
seoWhoAiCites: Who AI cites in the SERP:
seoFactGaps: Extractable fact gaps (key for AI citation)
seoSetSub: Keys for the SEO Tools tab (Outline Generator and Content Analysis). SERP analysis is the main cost of the pipeline. Connect any provider and switch between them.
seoSetTip: All keys are stored in the browser only and sent directly to the provider. Minimum to start: one SERP key (Serper) + an AI provider above. Firecrawl is optional — without it scraping uses direct fetch.
seoSetHintSerper: Cheap and simple (~$0.30–1 / 1000). Best choice to start.
seoSetInstrSerper: Sign up at serper.dev → Dashboard → API Key. You get 2500 free requests to start.
seoSetHintDfs: SERP (Google + Bing) and Ahrefs-like metrics in one API. Paste the Base64 token from the dashboard, or login:password.
seoSetInstrDfs: Create an account at dataforseo.com → API Access. Easiest: copy the ready Base64 token and paste it here. Or paste login:password (your email and API password) — both work.
seoSetHintFc: Paid scraper: bypasses anti-bot, returns clean markdown. Used when direct fetch fails.
seoSetInstrFc: Sign up at firecrawl.dev → Dashboard → API Keys. Without this key scraping uses direct fetch (fails on ~30–50% of sites).
seoPolHelpWhat: An editorial policy is a set of permanent rules for the generator — brand, audience, voice & tone, structure, quality requirements, banned words and compliance. On every generation it's injected into the prompt as a block the AI 'copywriter' must follow.
seoPolHelpWhy: So all your texts sound consistent, you don't re-explain requirements every time, quality stays controlled, and you can scale a network of sites quickly. Create a separate policy per content type or project.
seoPolHelpAffects: The active policy applies to BOTH structure and text generation. Banned words also drive the optional 'hard redaction' (replacing forbidden words with […]), and compliance keeps the model from inventing licenses or promising guarantees.
seoPolHelpTone: The tone inside the policy is the default. If you pick a tone at generation time it OVERRIDES the policy tone (written once — no conflict). A 'commercial' tone makes the structure selling-oriented; an 'informational' tone makes it a reference guide.
seoPolHelpTip: Be specific ('use personal examples and player cases', not 'write interestingly'), avoid contradictory rules ('be brief but detailed'), and don't skip compliance for regulated niches.
seoPolHubSub: Create editorial policy templates for your projects
seoPolEditorSub: Configure content generation rules for your brand
seoEeatCalloutDesc: Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trust — key ranking factors for YMYL content
seoEeatReqSub: How to demonstrate experience and expertise
seoEeatReqPh: e.g. State authorship, experience and credentials. Cite official sources.
seoBannedWordsPh: e.g. cheap, guaranteed, best in the world
seoGenAiSub: Provide information about your brand and style preferences to generate a comprehensive editorial policy.
seoGenAiAddUrls: Add page URLs from your website (e.g. About Us, Home)
seoGenAiUrlsPh: One URL per line — they'll be scraped for grounding
seoGenAiDescPh: Describe the brand: what it does, values, USP…
seoGenAiAddSample: Add sample text to analyze style
seoGenAiSamplePh: Paste a sample text — tone, voice and formatting will be inferred from it…
seoGenAiNichePh: Niche or project, e.g. "home massage in Greece"
seoJsonExportSub: Copy or download the policy configuration as JSON
seoCustomerProfilePh: Who reads this — needs, pains, context
seoAuthorPersonaPh: e.g. experienced local driver, certified nutritionist
seoCfgAddCompUrlSub: Add a competitor that isn't in the SERP yet (e.g. a fast-rising site). It joins the list above and is scraped together with the rest.
seoCfgManualSub: Paste competitor content when scraping fails
seoModelSub: Used for outline and text generation. Stronger model = richer EAV output.
seoModelNote: Exact IDs depend on your active AI provider. Leave default if unsure.
seoModelNoProviders: Add an AI provider key above first.
seoModelLive: Models load live from your configured providers.
seoModelErrFetch: Couldn't load some providers — check the key.
seoHistorySub: All your generated outlines, texts and content analyses
seoHistEmpty: No tasks yet — generate an outline or run an analysis.
seoGenTextModalSub: Set parameters and generate the article
seoTextGenSub: Create SEO-optimized content from your policies and outlines
seoNoStructures: No saved outlines — generate a structure first.
seoCustomInstructionPh: Anything to add to the generation from your side…
seoFcDisclaimer: Model self-assessment of claim confidence, not source verification.
seoFcRealNote: Each section is searched live and verified against real sources (uses your SERP key). This takes a while.
seoFcNoSerpNote: No SERP key — running model-only assessment without sources.
seoAutoFactcheckDesc: Verify against sources automatically when a text task is opened (uses SERP quota).
seoAutoImagesDesc: Generate image prompts automatically (cheap, one model call).
seoFactSourcesDesc: More = more accurate but slower and costlier. 0 = SERP snippets only.
seoSourcesModeHint: For commercial / own-brand pages choose «no competitors» or «don't use». Slower when on (SERP + scrape).
seoHardRedactDesc: Replace policy banned words in the text with […] (for critical commercial pages). Can affect phrasing — review the result.
seoFactBearingDesc: Skip narrative/intro sections that have nothing to verify (prices, times, distances, numbers). Saves SERP queries with no quality loss. Recommended.
seoFactReuseDesc: Verify all sections against ONE shared set of top-SERP pages instead of a live search per section. Big cost saver, but niche facts may stay unconfirmed. Off = max-freshness live mode.
seoKwFeedNote: These real keywords are fed into the outline (volume-prioritised).
seoCitSub: Find where your brand/keyword is cited across the web + sentiment (GEO/AI-visibility)
seoCitInputPh: Brand or keyword, e.g. your company name
seoCitEmpty: No citations found for this query.
seoCaYourUrlHint: Enter the URL of YOUR existing content you want to analyze and improve
seoCaSelectCompetitorsHint: Pick competitor articles to compare against (3-10 recommended)
seoCaAddCustomHint: Add up to 5 competitor URLs that aren't in the SERP results above.
seoCaAddCustomMulti: You can add several URLs at once, one per line
seoCaAnalyzingHint: Content analysis usually takes 1-3 minutes. You can keep this tab open.
seoCaScatterAxes: X: Mentions | Y: Triplets | Size: Competitor Median
